heading: Planting flexibility
label: 7218 Planting flexibility
notes: Contract Payments for Wild Rice Acreage, , , provided that:Pub. L. 106–78, title VII, § 727113 Stat. 1164Oct. 22, 1999“None of the funds appropriated or otherwise available to the Department of Agriculture in fiscal year 2000 or thereafter may be used to administer the provision of contract payments to a producer under the Agricultural Market Transition Act ( et seq.) for contract acreage on which wild rice is planted unless the contract payment is reduced by an acre for each contract acre planted to wild rice.”7 U.S.C. 7201Similar provisions were contained in the following prior appropriations acts:, , , 2681–28.Pub. children.heading: Permitted crops
children.label: a Permitted crops
children.text: Subject to subsection (b), any commodity or crop may be planted on contract acreage on a farm.

children.heading: Limitations and exceptions regarding fruits and vegetables
children.label: b Limitations and exceptions regarding fruits and vegetables

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: 1 Limitations
children.text: The planting of fruits and vegetables (other than lentils, mung beans, and dry peas) shall be prohibited on contract acreage.

children.label: A
children.text: in any region in which there is a history of double-cropping of contract commodities with fruits or vegetables, as determined by the Secretary, in which case the double-cropping shall be permitted;

children.label: B
children.text: on a farm that the Secretary determines has a history of planting fruits or vegetables on contract acreage, except that a contract payment shall be reduced by an acre for each acre planted to the fruit or vegetable; or

children.label: i
children.text: the quantity planted may not exceed the producer’s average annual planting history of the fruit or vegetable in the 1991 through 1995 crop years (excluding any crop year in which no plantings were made), as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a contract payment shall be reduced by an acre for each acre planted to the fruit or vegetable.
